mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate cuts signal monetary easing and flight-to-safety demand, which compresses long-term discount rates and reduces 10-year Treasury yields. A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS over the prior 2 months predicts negative 10-year yield direction over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.81% as of Sept 2008 reflects emergency rate cuts during financial crisis
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle during financial stress
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 55% of 20-day forward windows when FEDFUNDS is declining, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment signals economic contraction and increased recession risk, triggering equity sell-offs and safe-haven demand for Treasuries, which drives 10-year yields downward. UNRATE crossing above 6.0% predicts negative 10-year yield direction within 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.1% as of Sept 2008, near crisis peak
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 60% of instances when UNRATE > 6.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Collapsing retail sales and housing starts signal demand destruction and deflationary pressure. Joint deterioration in RSAFS and HOUST below their 12-month moving averages predicts negative 10-year yield direction as investors flee to safety and inflation expectations collapse.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 5% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 895k units (Sept 2008), down sharply from 2006 peak of 2.3M
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 50% of triggered instances, or joint signal produces lower Rank IC than either series alone over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contraction signals supply-side weakness and recession confirmation. INDPRO declining below its 3-month trend predicts negative 10-year Treasury yields as growth expectations compress and safe-haven demand increases.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 55% of 5-day windows following INDPRO declines > 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment collapse signals severe labor market deterioration and recession deepening. PAYEMS declining > 200k month-over-month predicts negative 10-year Treasury yields as equity risk premiums widen and investors shift to duration.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137.3M (Sept 2008), declining sharply during crisis
regime_hint: labor market shock during financial crisis
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 60% of 20-day windows following PAYEMS declines > 200k, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation expectations collapse during deflationary shock, measured by CPIAUCSL deceleration, combined with emergency Fed easing (FEDFUNDS near zero). This joint signal predicts negative 10-year Treasury yields as real rates compress and nominal yields fall.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL growth rate falls below 1.5% annualized AND FEDFUNDS < 2.0%
regime_hint: deflationary shock with monetary emergency response
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 50% of 60-day windows when both conditions are met, or interaction signal produces lower Rank IC than single-series alternatives over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP contraction (GDPC1 declining quarter-over-quarter) signals recession confirmation and triggers flight-to-safety demand for long-duration Treasuries, predicting negative 10-year yield direction. This mechanism is regime-conditional: it activates only when GDPC1 growth turns negative.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth rate turns negative
regime_hint: recession regime with negative GDP growth
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 55% of 60-day windows following negative GDPC1 quarters, or Rank IC < 0.07 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts collapse (HOUST < 900k) combined with retail sales weakness (RSAFS declining) signals demand destruction across both residential and consumer sectors. This interaction predicts negative 10-year Treasury yields as growth expectations collapse and duration demand increases.
regime_hint: demand shock during financial crisis with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 50% of 20-day windows when both demand conditions are met, or three-series interaction produces lower Rank IC than two-series subsets over 2-year rolling windows.
